name: Sanctuary of Foix and Pèlags del Foix Loop
name_original: Santuario de Foix, Rio Foix – Ruta Circular
description: Hike the Santuario de Foix, Rio Foix – Circular Route, a moderate 5.2-mile trail with panoramic views and river pools.
region_intro: Hiking around Torrelles De Foix offers diverse natural landscapes in the Alt Penedès region of Barcelona, Spain. The area features a blend of mountains, river ecosystems, and historical sites, with the Foix River and its tributary, the Pontons stream, flowing through. Notable features include the natural springs of Les Dous and the Pèlags del Foix, a series of natural pools. The municipality's highest point, Turó dels Quatre Termes, provides panoramic views of the surrounding Penedès plain. Best hiking trails around Torrelles De Foix The most popular hiking route is Sanctuary of Foix and Pèlags del Foix Loop, a 5.2 miles (8.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 26 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects the Santuari de la Mare de Déu de Foix with the tranquil natural pools of the Foix River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Camí les Dous, Sant Genís, Torrelles de Foix – Circular Route, an easy 2.9 miles (4.7 km) path. This trail explores the area around the 35 natural springs of Les Dous. Local hikers also love the Pools of the Foix River – Foix Sanctuary loop from el Cusconar, a 9.2 miles (14.8 km) trail leading through river ecosystems and past historical sites, often completed in about 4 hours 18 minutes. Hiking around Torrelles De Foix is defined by river ecosystems, natural springs, and panoramic viewpoints over vineyards. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Torrelles De Foix are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Torrelles De Foix's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Torrelles De Foix?
A: There are 19 hiking trails around Torrelles De Foix, offering a variety of experiences from easy strolls to more challenging routes. These trails explore the region's diverse landscapes, including river ecosystems, natural springs, and panoramic viewpoints over vineyards.

Q: What types of natural features can I expect to see on the trails?
A: Torrelles De Foix is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the 35 natural springs of Les Dous, and during rainy seasons, impressive waterfalls near the Pontons stream. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3028298" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pèlags del Foix&lt;/a&gt;, a series of unique natural pools along the Foix River, are also a prominent feature. Many trails also offer expansive views from elevated points like Turó dels Quatre Termes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Torrelles De Foix?
A: Yes, Torrelles De Foix offers several easy trails suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939040832/cami-les-dous-sant-genis-torrelles-de-foix-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camí les Dous, Sant Genís, Torrelles de Foix – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.9-mile path that explores the area around the natural springs of Les Dous, which also features a picnic area. There are 6 easy routes in total, perfect for a leisurely family outing.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Torrelles De Foix are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular circular routes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e946255915/sanctuary-of-foix-and-pelags-del-foix-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Foix and Pèlags del Foix Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939040832/cami-les-dous-sant-genis-torrelles-de-foix-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camí les Dous, Sant Genís, Torrelles de Foix – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;. These routes are designed to showcase the region's highlights efficiently.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I visit while hiking?
A: The region combines natural beauty with historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5344519" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuari de la Mare de Déu de Foix&lt;/a&gt;, perched on a hill, is a significant religious site offering panoramic views. You can also find historical paths like the Camí Ral, and explore nearby castles such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3934046" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Martí Sarroca Castle&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4418293" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of La Granada&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of the rich heritage of the Penedès region.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Torrelles De Foix?
A: The trails in Torrelles De Foix are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the tranquility of the natural pools, and the stunning panoramic views, especially from the Santuari de la Mare de Déu de Foix.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls or natural springs?
A: Absolutely. The area is known for its hydrological features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939040832/cami-les-dous-sant-genis-torrelles-de-foix-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camí les Dous, Sant Genís, Torrelles de Foix – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt; specifically explores the area around the 35 natural springs of Les Dous. During wetter periods, these springs can create impressive waterfalls along the Pontons stream.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Torrelles De Foix?
A: The region's lushness, fed by the Foix River and Pontons stream, makes spring and early summer ideal for enjoying the vibrant greenery and comfortable temperatures. Autumn is also beautiful, especially with the vineyards changing colors. If you wish to see the waterfalls at their most impressive, visiting after a period of rain is recommended.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Torrelles De Foix?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in natural areas like Torrelles De Foix. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near agricultural land, and to clean up after them. Specific restrictions might apply to certain protected areas or private properties, so always look for local signage.

Q: Where can I find trails with the best panoramic views?
A: For breathtaking panoramic views, consider routes that ascend to higher points. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e946255915/sanctuary-of-foix-and-pelags-del-foix-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Foix and Pèlags del Foix Loop&lt;/a&gt; includes the Santuari de la Mare de Déu de Foix, which offers privileged vistas of the Penedès plain. The Turó dels Quatre Termes, the highest point in the municipality, also provides expansive views of the surrounding area.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more demanding experience, there are challenging routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14898023" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pools of the Foix River – Foix Sanctuary loop from el Cusconar&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 9.2-mile trail that takes about 4 hours 18 minutes to complete, leading through diverse river ecosystems and past historical sites. In total, there is 1 difficult route and 12 moderate routes for more experienced hikers.
tips: This sanctuary is Romanesque in style and around it are preserved the remains of the castle of Foix. They have built a viewpoint to be able to see the entire Penedés, which reaches the sea. Nearby there is a water source.
Spectacular corner where you can enjoy the River Foix, and even be able to cool off, respecting the fauna and flora that nature offers us. The sounds of nature will make you relax and unwind.
Here you can see the ruins of a mill. The building remains with two clearly visible corner pieces where the ashlar has fitted, the rest of the mill has succumbed due to the floods.

The Font del Rector is about 300 meters from the sanctuary of Foix, sunk below ground level, protected by three stone walls. On the central wall there is a push-button faucet. Above the spring is the mine, dug out of the mountain. It is accessed through an opening and some steps and inside there is an iron tube that channels the water, when it exists, towards the fountain. Next to the entrance there is a tile with the name of the fountain and a cute owl on a log.

faqs.question: How long does the Santuario de Foix, Rio Foix – Circular Route typically take?
faqs.answer: This moderate circular route usually takes between 2 hours 26 minutes and 3 hours 15 minutes to complete. The komoot tour data estimates it at approximately 2 hours 26 minutes.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: The route is classified as moderate, requiring good fitness and sure-footedness. While mostly accessible, some sections involve a somewhat steep descent towards the Foix River and potential scrambling over rocks. It's generally considered suitable for older children or those accustomed to hiking.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts and ends at the Santuari de Foix, which is perched on a 660-meter-high hill. Parking is usually available near the sanctuary, serving as a convenient trailhead.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable features. The route begins and ends at the historic Sanctuary of Foix and Castle Ruins, offering panoramic views. As you descend, you'll reach the scenic Pèlags del Foix, a series of natural pools. Other points of interest include the Ruins of the Molinot and the Font del Rector Spring.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking. In summer, the Pèlags del Foix can be a refreshing spot for a swim. Wild thyme blooms add to the beauty in spring.

faqs.question: Is the Santuario de Foix, Rio Foix – Circular Route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is generally dog-friendly. However, be prepared for sections that might require some scrambling over rocks and a river crossing, which could be challenging for some dogs depending on water levels. Always keep your dog under control, especially near wildlife or livestock.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to hike this circular route.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and views can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse and picturesque scenery. From the Santuari de Foix, you'll get superb panoramic views of the entire Penedès region, extending to the sea, and even the majestic Montserrat massif in the distance. As you descend, the landscape transitions to vineyards, agricultural fields, and the tranquil Mediterranean environment of the Foix River valley with its natural pools.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. It begins with a descent from the sanctuary, which can be steep in places. You'll encounter mostly accessible paths, but some sections may require scrambling over rocks. The route includes paths along the river, and the return often follows tracks and potentially some road sections back to the sanctuary.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or water sources along the route?
faqs.answer: While there are no dedicated facilities like cafes directly on the trail itself, the Font del Rector Spring is a natural water source mentioned along the route. It's always advisable to carry sufficient water, especially on warmer days. Amenities might be available near the Santuari de Foix at the start/end point.

faqs.question: What should I know about crossing the Foix River?
faqs.answer: The route involves crossing the Foix River. This can present a challenge depending on recent rainfall and water levels. Be prepared for potentially wet feet or needing to find stable stepping stones. Always assess the conditions before attempting to cross.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of Foix and Castle Ruins
